generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance PointCloudLiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "3‑D point‑cloud sensor on the teleoperated 1/10‑scale autonomous vehicle chassis providing raw point clouds for clustering and tracking"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleSpeedSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle‑state sensor measuring speed, used by ACC monitoring UI and ACC computation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleSpeedDataTransmitted
]

instance VehiclePositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle‑state sensor measuring position, used by ACC monitoring UI and ACC computation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehiclePositionDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor reporting motor status (rpm, torque) for actuation feedback"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringServoPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor reporting steering‑servo position for vehicle state visualization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringServoPositionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Data transmitted from sensors
instance PointCloudDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PointCloudLiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleSpeedDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleSpeedSensor
]

instance VehiclePositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehiclePositionSensor
]

instance MotorStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStatusSensor
]

instance SteeringServoPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoPositionSensor
]

// C5: Data instances
instance PointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw point‑cloud dataset streamed to the Digital Twin for clustering and tracking"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2NodePointCloudProcessing
]

instance VehicleSpeedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current vehicle speed value used by ACC algorithm"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleSpeedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2NodeACCComputation
]

instance VehiclePositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current vehicle position (x, y, heading) used by ACC algorithm and UI"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehiclePositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2NodeACCComputation
]

instance MotorStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor operational metrics (rpm, torque) fed back to control loops"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2NodeACCComputation
]

instance SteeringServoPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering servo angle used for visualizing vehicle state"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringServoPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2NodeACCComputation
]

// C10: Models/Data (Behavioral models)
instance ACCBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ACC behavioral model that identifies lead vehicle, computes desired speed based on inter‑vehicle distance, and returns speed command"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2NodeACCComputation
]

instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric point‑cloud clustering and tracking model generating object tracks and projected driving path"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2NodePointCloudProcessing
]

// C11: Enablers (ROS2 nodes, storage, UI, communication)
instance ROS2NodeACCComputation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 package implementing the ACC computation node, executing the ACCBehaviorModel in the cloud"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ROS2NodePointCloudProcessing : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 package implementing point‑cloud clustering and tracking node, realizing the PointCloudClusteringModel"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component persisting collected sensor data and computed results for later reporting and analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance UIVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Graphical UI front‑end that visualizes real‑time vehicle state, object tracks, and projected path, and allows user interaction"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance CommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low‑latency Wi‑Fi UDP link enabling bi‑directional data exchange between Physical Twin, Digital Twin, and User entities"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data Monitoring service provides real‑time UI display, data viewing, saving, and report generation"
    DTDFVocab:provides ViewVehicleStateInsight, GenerateReportAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computation Offloading service runs the ACC algorithm in the Digital Twin and returns speed commands"
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputeDesiredSpeedAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote Management service lets operators enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, and issue emergency braking"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, DisableACCAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

// C17: Insights / Actions
instance ViewVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight allowing operators to view current vehicle state, object tracks, and projected path in the UI"
]

instance GenerateReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to generate a PDF/CSV report from collected sensor data and computed results"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ComputeDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action that computes the desired speed based on lead‑vehicle detection and inter‑vehicle distance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to enable the Adaptive Cruise Control functionality"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DisableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to disable the Adaptive Cruise Control functionality"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action allowing the operator to set a target cruising speed"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Safety‑critical action to trigger an emergency brake command to the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin Entity generates control commands for the physical vehicle through the Actuation FE (Motor Actuation, Steering Servo Actuation). Commands include enabling/disabling ACC, setting a desired speed, and an emergency‑brake request as described in the remote‑management service. These commands are transmitted over the same Wi‑Fi/UDP link used for inter‑entity communication and are executed by the actuators of the Physical Twin Entity. After actuation, vehicle‑state feedback is sent back to the DT, closing the loop. The documents do not detail the exact message format, authentication, validation, conflict‑resolution or explicit execution acknowledgements."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT requires the physical and digital components to share a common notion of time with synchronization accuracy of ≤100 ms (as mandated by safety‑critical vehicular standards). The communication channel between vehicle and DT must also provide low end‑to‑end latency sufficient for real‑time ACC control (e.g., to send desired speed and emergency braking commands within the control cycle), though a specific latency bound is not stated."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "An entity‑based reference architecture is realized by mapping each functional entity to a ROS 2 package; each package contains one or more ROS 2 nodes implementing the entity’s functionality. The Physical Twin Entity hosts sensor acquisition, point‑cloud clustering, and actuation; the Digital Twin Entity hosts the computational model of the ACC algorithm; the User Entity provides a 3D visualization and control panel UI. Computation offloading moves the ACC model to cloud resources, while low‑latency network links coordinate state updates between the physical twin and the DT, achieving full system orchestration."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DTS uses a ROS 2 middleware for intra‑component communication, with each functional element packaged as a ROS 2 node. Inter‑entity communication (Physical Twin ↔ Digital Twin ↔ User) is carried out over a Wi‑Fi network using UDP at the transport layer. The system is required to support low end‑to‑end latency suitable for safety‑critical ACC, and a clock‑synchronisation accuracy of 100 ms is mandated. No explicit bandwidth, reliability redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols (e.g., TLS, authentication) are mentioned in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Computation off‑loading of the ACC algorithm is performed on external compute resources referred to as “the cloud”, while the remaining services run on the hardware components that host the Physical Twin Entity, Digital Twin Entity and User Entity. Each functional element is implemented as an individual ROS 2 package, deployed on separate hardware platforms (on‑board vehicle hardware for the Physical Twin, external compute for the Digital Twin, and a user workstation for the UI). The documents do not give quantitative CPU, memory or storage requirements, nor describe scalability mechanisms, deployment models (container, VM) or availability/redundancy provisions."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with the physical vehicle is achieved via ROS 2 middleware over a low‑latency network link, enabling data collection, command transmission, and remote management. The architecture adheres to the ISO 23247 standard, which permits use of standards such as OPC UA for interoperability, although the present implementation relies on ROS 2 for data exchange."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system follows the ISO 23247 standard (parts 1‑4), using its entity‑based reference architecture to organise the Digital Twin, Data Collection and Device Control, and User entities. The standard’s independence from specific data formats or protocols allowed the authors to employ domain‑specific technologies such as ROS2 for middleware and to consider OPC Unified Architecture as a possible protocol. For automotive safety‑critical contexts, the authors recommend extending compliance with ISO 26262. The architecture also aligns with AUTOSAR principles, emphasizing hardware‑agnostic software components that can be retrofitted into existing automotive software stacks."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The case study notes that the ISO 23247 reference architecture does not explicitly address safety‑critical aspects; it recommends promoting safety to a first‑class concern by integrating established automotive safety standards such as ISO 26262‑1, conducting systematic risk assessments, and defining fail‑safe mechanisms for functional safety. Although security is mentioned as an area needing further research, no specific cybersecurity protocols, authentication methods, or access‑control mechanisms are detailed in the provided documents."
]
